content.text:
Memory stage: NoteConstructor v1.
        Current time: <instant>
        Timezone: Asia/Jerusalem
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Router decision: MIXED
        Router memory types: CLAIM, NOTE, SOURCE, ENTITY
        Router reason: Document ingest uses deterministic document truth route; stable document facts are extracted as claims, notes/sources keep richer context, and action items stay explicit-only.

        Stage instructions:
        You are NoteConstructor v1 for a long-term AI agent.

Goal:
Construct reusable semantic notes from TARGET_MESSAGE when the material is contextual rather than a clean atomic fact.

A note is for decisions, rationale, design direction, plans, hypotheses, lessons, trade-offs, troubleshooting procedures, and compact document/context digests.
A note must add reusable meaning. It must not be a generic summary of the target turn.

Return JSON:
{
  "notes": [
    {
      "title": "short English title",
      "summary": "self-contained English semantic fragment",
      "note_type": "decision | direction | hypothesis | plan | lesson | doc_digest | context",
      "scope_json": {
        "kind": "global | conversation | entity | environment | document | project",
        "text": "human-readable applicability boundary",
        "basis": "explicit | inferred | summarized | imported"
      },
      "entity_refs": [
        {"entity_id": "resolved-entity-id", "role": "primary | secondary | mentioned | owner | subject"}
      ],
      "keywords": ["short search keyword"],
      "tags": ["short_tag"],
      "candidate_claim_hints": [],
      "confidence": 0.0,
      "importance": 1,
      "valid_from": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "valid_to": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "evidence_quote": "exact substring from TARGET_MESSAGE",
      "evidence_kind": "direct | summarized | imported | inferred | derived_from_note",
      "evidence_reason": "why this source supports the note",
      "rationale": "why this should be a note, not a claim"
    }
  ]
}

Rules:
- Write title, summary, keywords, tags, evidence_reason, and rationale in English.
- Preserve proper names, product names, repo names, file names, exact commands, exact identifiers, dates, and quoted terms.
- Use only entity IDs listed in Resolved entities.
- Never write raw labels such as "user", "assistant", "project", or "document" into entity_refs. If a note is about the user, use the resolved USER entity id from Resolved entities.
- For project-level notes without a more specific subject, use the resolved namespace/project subject entity.
- Use TARGET_MESSAGE as the only evidence. Earlier messages are context only.
- evidence_quote must be an exact short substring copied from TARGET_MESSAGE source data.
- Do not construct notes from greetings, phatic chatter, pure questions, one-off commands with no durable rationale, or memory mechanics discussion that contains no reusable project/user decision.
- Do not construct notes from a single weak uncertain factual observation when source recall is enough and there is no rationale, decision, plan, lesson, or reusable analysis.
- Do not duplicate a claim candidate as a note when a precise claim is enough.
- Do use a note when the target captures "why", "how", "we decided", "current approach", trade-offs, working agreements, troubleshooting order, or a design direction.
- If TARGET_MESSAGE is a document or document section, preserve document title/source/section names and create section-scoped notes for important definitions, component roles, workflows, policies, and lists.
- For dense technical documents and prompt packs, prefer several strong section-scoped notes over one broad document summary. Keep only reusable notes; do not summarize every paragraph.
- For one document section, return at most two notes. If a section defines one compact component or policy, return one note.
- If source metadata force_memory_write=true, create a note when the content is reusable context but not a clean claim/action item/profile update.
- For named components, include the exact component names in title, summary, keywords, or tags so later retrieval can find the component role.
- A troubleshooting/procedure note is allowed, but do not create an action item unless the target creates a commitment.
- Keep each note self-contained: future recall should understand it without the original conversation.
- Keep summary concise: usually one or two sentences.
- Prefer zero notes over broad summary spam.
- Return valid JSON only.

        Relevant persisted memory for dedup/context only:
        - note hot-path:note:<0> DOC_DIGEST; Recommended pipeline routes routed writes through canonicalization, specialized updaters, reconciliation, and storage; In the recommended architecture, Memory Router outcomes (noop/direct_structured_write/note_write/mixed) feed Write-Time Retrieval and Entity Canonicalizer, then specialized components (Claim Extractor, Task Updater, Profile Updater, Note Constructor) and reconcilers write to Postgres + pgvector, with background consolidation/repair/retention workers and runtime retrieval-answer loop.; scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 4. Рекомендуемая архитектура' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
- note hot-path:note:<1> DOC_DIGEST; `v3` memory architecture uses four axes to avoid brittle or dump-like memory; In `v3`, memory is organized along four axes—Representation (Source/Claim/Note/Profile/Task/Episode), Lifecycle (write→reconcile→consolidate→repair→retain/archive/delete), Retrieval policy (claim-first/note-first/mixed/evidence-first with bounded budgets), and Scope & lineage (scope_json/scope_text/source refs/origin note/memory runs). The document states all four are necessary to prevent transcript-dump or brittle fact-store failure modes.; scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 2. Центральная архитектурная идея' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
- claim hot-path:claim:<0> has_constraint; Consolidation promotes mature notes into claims, tasks, profile updates, or episodes when the signal becomes strong enough.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section 3 maintenance paths
- claim hot-path:claim:<1> has_constraint; Tier A core in-context memory should include a short user profile, active tasks, a very short session summary, and system-level behavior preferences.; scope=Applies to the architecture described in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 5.
- claim hot-path:claim:<2> has_constraint; Tier A core in-context memory should not include all claims, all notes, raw transcript, or long lists of facts.; scope=Applies to the architecture described in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 5.
- claim hot-path:claim:<3> has_goal; The system goal is for the memory layer to support updating knowledge rather than only accumulating it.; scope=Applies to the architecture described in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md (section 1).
- claim hot-path:claim:<4> has_constraint; Repair addresses memory degradation including duplicate clusters, stale items, contradictory active items, note spam, profile drift, and bad merges.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section 3 maintenance paths
- claim hot-path:claim:<5> has_goal; The system goal is for the memory layer to store facts, preferences, tasks, rationale, and evolving plans.; scope=Applies to the architecture described in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md (section 1).
- claim hot-path:claim:<6> has_constraint; Both claim and note should be written when material contains explicit facts or tasks plus rationale or direction.; scope=Applies to agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section 7 guidance
- claim hot-path:claim:<7> has_goal; The system goal is for the memory layer to support aging, archiving, and repairing memory.; scope=Applies to the architecture described in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md (section 1).
- claim hot-path:claim:<8> has_constraint; A claim should be written when information can be compared with other claims under predicate policy.; scope=Applies to agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section 7 guidance
- note hot-path:note:<2> DOC_DIGEST; Storage tiers define a five-layer memory architecture; Section 5 defines five storage tiers with distinct roles: Tier A for compact in-context prompt blocks, Tier B for typed long-term records, Tier C for semantic note-layer context, Tier D for immutable raw evidence, and Tier E for retrieval indexes across memory objects.; scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 5. Storage tiers .. 6. Structured scope' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
- claim hot-path:claim:<9> has_goal; The system goal is to have a memory layer for an AI agent that survives beyond a single session.; scope=Applies to the architecture described in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md (section 1).
- claim hot-path:claim:<10> has_constraint; Tier D raw evidence is an immutable or near-immutable layer that stores chat turns, tool outputs, and imported records.; scope=Applies to the architecture described in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 5.
- claim hot-path:claim:<11> has_constraint; Tier C rich note memory serves as an intermediate semantic layer for design direction, rationale, local conclusions, plan fragments, document digests, and lessons.; scope=Applies to the architecture described in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 5.
- claim hot-path:claim:<12> has_goal; The system goal is for the memory layer to explain where a memory came from.; scope=Applies to the architecture described in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md (section 1).
- claim hot-path:claim:<13> has_constraint; The memory system must not convert every turn into permanent knowledge.; scope=Applies to the architecture described in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md (section 1).
- claim hot-path:claim:<14> has_constraint; A note should be written when information is useful for future retrieval.; scope=Applies to agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section 7 guidance
- claim hot-path:claim:<15> has_constraint; A note should be written when information is too context-rich or too early for a hard fact.; scope=Applies to agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section 7 guidance

        Resolved entities:
        - mention=User-facing runtime; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<0>; type=SERVICE; name=User-facing runtime; reason=A concrete architectural component described as a durable system part; no safe existing match in candidates.
- mention=Memory worker / manager; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<1>; type=SERVICE; name=Memory worker / manager; reason=Distinct named architectural component with clear responsibilities; not matched by existing entities.
- mention=Storage layer; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<2>; type=SERVICE; name=Storage layer; reason=Clearly defined durable architectural component; no existing candidate corresponds to it.
- mention=Task Updater; action=LINK_EXISTING; id=entity:<3>; type=existing; name=Task Updater; reason=Existing candidate exactly matches this service name and type in retrieval context.
- mention=current project; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<4>; type=PROJECT; name=Current project; reason=Default current-project subject for claims without a more specific resolved subject

        Retrieval plan context:

Document title: 01. Architecture
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
Document section: 01. Architecture / 9. Главная мысль: update не должен быть append-only .. 10. Разделение ответственности
Document lines: 277-328

## 9. Главная мысль: update не должен быть append-only

На записи у системы четыре обязательные задачи:

1. понять, стоит ли вообще писать память
2. найти похожие или конфликтующие items
3. превратить input в typed candidates
4. применить policy:
   - `insert`
   - `update`
   - `supersede`
   - `retract`
   - `noop`

Если пропустить retrieval/reconciliation, база быстро зарастает:
- дублями
- stale preferences
- conflicting notes
- task explosion
- summary spam

## 10. Разделение ответственности

### 10.1 User-facing runtime
Делает:
- диалог
- tool orchestration
- read-time retrieval
- cheap routing / escalation

Он не должен тащить на себе тяжелый reconciliation и repair.

### 10.2 Memory worker / manager
Делает:
- routing
- retrieval-before-update
- canonicalization
- note/claim extraction
- reconciliation
- consolidation
- repair
- retention

### 10.3 Storage layer
Отвечает за:
- immutable evidence
- typed tables
- transactional updates
- lineage
- indexes
- audit trail

responseFormat.description: Construct contextual memory note candidates from one target message.
responseFormat.name: memory_note_constructor

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

## 9. Главная мысль: update не должен быть append-only

На записи у системы четыре обязательные задачи:

1. понять, стоит ли вообще писать память
2. найти похожие или конфликтующие items
3. превратить input в typed candidates
4. применить policy:
   - `insert`
   - `update`
   - `supersede`
   - `retract`
   - `noop`

Если пропустить retrieval/reconciliation, база быстро зарастает:
- дублями
- stale preferences
- conflicting notes
- task explosion
- summary spam

## 10. Разделение ответственности

### 10.1 User-facing runtime
Делает:
- диалог
- tool orchestration
- read-time retrieval
- cheap routing / escalation

Он не должен тащить на себе тяжелый reconciliation и repair.

### 10.2 Memory worker / manager
Делает:
- routing
- retrieval-before-update
- canonicalization
- note/claim extraction
- reconciliation
- consolidation
- repair
- retention

### 10.3 Storage layer
Отвечает за:
- immutable evidence
- typed tables
- transactional updates
- lineage
- indexes
- audit trail

responseFormat.description: Construct contextual memory note candidates from one target message.

Architecture / 9. Главная мысль: update не должен быть append-only' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md"},"summary":"The architecture defines memory writes as an update workflow, not blind append: decide whether to write, retrieve similar/conflicting items, produce typed candidates, and apply policies (`insert`, `update`, `supersede`, `retract`, `noop`). Skipping retrieval/reconciliation causes quality degradation such as duplicates, stale preferences, conflicts, task explosion, and summary spam.","tags":["architecture","memory-write-path","doc_section_9"],"title":"Update path is not append-only and requires retrieval/reconciliation","valid_from":"1970-01-01T00:00:00Z","valid_to":null},{"candidate_claim_hints":[],"confidence":0.96,"entity_refs":[{"entity_id":"entity:<0>","role":"primary"},{"entity_id":"entity:<1>","role":"primary"},{"entity_id":"entity:<2>","role":"primary"}],"evidence_kind":"direct","evidence_quote":"## 10. Разделение ответственности","evidence_reason":"The section explicitly defines component boundaries and assigned duties for the three named components.","importance":5,"keywords":["User-facing runtime","Memory worker / manager","Storage layer","separation of responsibilities"],"note_type":"doc_digest","rationale":"This is a durable component-role mapping and design direction used for future reasoning, not just one atomic claim.","scope_json":{"basis":"imported","kind":"document","text":"Applies to section '01. Architecture / 10. Разделение ответственности' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md"},"summary":"The design separates concerns: User-facing runtime handles dialog, tool orchestration, read-time retrieval, and cheap routing/escalation; Memory worker / manager performs heavier memory-update operations (routing, retrieval-before-update, canonicalization, extraction, reconciliation, consolidation, repair, retention); Storage layer provides immutable evidence, typed tables, transactional updates, lineage, indexes, and audit trail.","tags":["architecture","component_roles","doc_section_10"],"title":"Responsibility split across User-facing runtime, Memory worker / manager, and Storage layer","valid_from":"1970-01-01T00:00:00Z","valid_to":null}]}
